core_mechanism: Divide-and-conquer reconstruction of a globally-defined space partition from overlapping local subdomains, exploiting a locally-checkable validity certificate (the empty-circumsphere / empty-region condition of a Delaunay tessellation) so that each element computed within a tile is provably identical to the one in the global structure, followed by deduplication and boundary stitching of the tiles into a single consistent whole.
brief_reason: The abstract decomposes the volume into overlapping blocks whose tessellations are filtered by a circumsphere criterion 'retaining only globally valid tetrahedra' and then merged via core filtering, deduplication, and boundary stitching, which is a local-certificate-based tile-and-stitch scheme for building a global structure that no single tile could compute.
